channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Arms deliveries and Africa Corps training: How Russia is bolstering its presence in Madagascar
item.description: From deliveries of drones and armoured vehicles to training sessions led by Russian Africa Corps instructors, security cooperation between Madagascar and Russia has intensified since the October 2025 coup. Russia’s growing influence is expanding into other sectors as well, raising questions within parts of Malagasy civil society.
item.source: © Russian Embassy in Madagascar / Presidency of the Refoundation of the Republic of Madagascar
item.dc:creator: The FRANCE 24 Observers

item.title: Venice Biennale: Boycotts and threats over participation of Russia & Israel
item.description: The Venice Biennale previewed its 61st and most chaotic edition ever on Tuesday, just days after the unprecedented resignation of its jury over the participation of Israel and Russia undermined the very structure of the world’s oldest contemporary art exhibition.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US suspends Hormuz operation: Washington believes close to deal with Iran
item.description: Kethevane Gorjestani gives us her analysis.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iranian and Chinese FMS meet as Beijing urges countries to maintain ceasefire
item.description: Yena Lee, France24 correspondent in Beijing China, has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Injured Mbappe faces backlash over Sardinia trip before Clasico
item.description: Sidelined with a hamstring injury, Real Madrid striker Kylian Mbappe has sparked anger among a section of the club's supporters after a holiday in Sardinia, triggering a fresh media storm ahead of Sunday's Clasico against Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Donald Trump renews pressure and plans to hike tariffs on EU cars
item.description: Car parts makers in Europe are ‌facing renewed pressure as Trump last week ⁠announced a tariff increase on cars and trucks from the European Union ​to 25% from the previously agreed 15%, saying the bloc had not complied with its trade deal ‌with Washington.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads to Spain to dock in Canary Islands
item.description: A luxury cruise ship that ​has been hit by an outbreak of the deadly hantavirus was preparing to travel from Cape Verde towards Europe on Wednesday after the Spanish government gave permission for it to dock in the Canary Islands. Sarah Morris has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Kyiv says Russia broke unilateral ceasefire with drone and missile attacks
item.description: Kyiv said on Wednesday that Russia had violated a unilateral ceasefire called by Ukraine by attacking Ukrainian cities with 108 combat drones and three missiles overnight.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi 'between life and death'
item.description: Jailed Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi is fighting for her life after being hospitalised under guard for the last five days with a heart condition.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 🔴 Live: Hantavirus-infected patient hospitalised in Zurich as three more 'evacuated' from cruise ship
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Live: Hantavirus-infected patient hospitalised in Zurich as three more 'evacuated' from cruise ship
item.description: A former passenger on a cruise ship stricken by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has been hospitalised in Zurich and has tested positive for the virus, the Swiss health ministry said Wednesday. Three other passengers, believed to have contracted the virus, have also been "evacuated" from the ship, currently anchored off the coast of Cape Verde, according to the World Health Organization. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: US-Iran conflict reaches painful stalemate: US will respond if fired on
item.description: Fraser Jackson, France24 correspondent in Washington DC, has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Putin said to be 'hiding in bunkers' as his popularity erodes
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, May 6, 2026: Russian President Vladimir Putin's popularity is eroding amid economic difficulties and internet restrictions. But first, a report outlining recommendations following a parliamentary inquiry into French public broadcasting is on the front page of several French papers. Also, Russia's participation at the Biennale in Venice has cast a shadow ahead of the opening of the international art exhibition. Finally, French papers look forward to the second leg of the Champions League semi-final.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: Evacuation of three people from hantavirus-hit cruise ship 'ongoing': WHO
item.description: South African health authorities said Wednesday they have identified the Andes strain of hantavirus, which may be passed from person to person, in two passengers who were on the cruise ship that has an outbreak of the rare infection.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Russia launches deadly attack in Ukraine: At least 28 killed
item.description: Philip Turle, France24 International Affairs Editor, gives us his analysis.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Venice Biennale faces outcry over Israel and Russia's participation
item.description: The world's largest contemporary art exhibition, which takes place every two years in the Italian canal city, features artists from several nations in conflict, including Ukraine, Israel and the United States -- although Iran has cancelled its plans to attend. Seema Gupta has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi in push to revise pacifist constitution
item.description: Japanese PM Takaichi in push to revise pacifist constitution as regional tensions rise
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US pauses Hormuz escorts, Donald Trump says progress on Iran deal
item.description: The United States will pause escorting commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz barely a day after it began doing so, President Donald Trump said, citing a desire to reach a peace deal with Iran.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Three infected people taken off hantavirus-hit cruise ship for treatment
item.description: Three people infected with hantavirus are being taken off a cruise ship stricken by the deadly disease while the ship was anchored off the coast of Cape Verde, the World Health Organization said Wednesday. Health officials confirmed the presence of the rare Andes strain, the only known form of the virus capable of human-to-human transmission.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Samsung Electronics reaches $1 trillion market cap thanks to surging demand for AI chips
item.description: The world's largest maker of memory chips, South Korea's Samsung Electronics, has seen its valuation reach $1 trillion this Wednesday. The company's profits have soared thanks to strong demand for the chips used to power artificial intelligence systems. Also in this edition: French oil major TotalEnergies says it will have to scrap caps on fuel prices if it faces a windfall tax on its profits in France.
item.dc:creator: Charles PELLEGRIN

item.title: 'Unconscionable to keep them on cruise ship’: WHOs Gostin warns against repeating COVID-era mistakes
item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Lawrence Gostin, Author, Director of the WHO Center on Global Health Law. His analysis highlights the legal and ethical dilemmas surrounding a cruise ship carrying suspected hantavirus cases off the coast of Cape Verde. Drawing direct parallels with the traumatic memory of cruise ships stranded during the COVID-19 pandemic, Gostin argues that the international community has already learned, at enormous human cost, the dangers of confining passengers at sea without adequate medical care or disembarkation plans.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: How unbreakable is Iran's regime? Tehran defiant despite deepening hardship for citizens
item.description: Iran's economy may be teetering on the brink of collapse, but does that really threaten its regime? Monday's flare-up of hostilities across the Strait of Hormuz makes clear that oil's not about to start flowing freely again. Tehran and Washington are both signalling they can bear the pain of lost revenue and inflation, with Iran arguably taking that brinkmanship further.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Arsenal to play first Champions League final in 20 years
item.description: Arsenal have reached the Champions League final for the first time since 2006 following their 1-0 victory over Atletico Madrid on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Ukraine reports Russian drone, missile attacks after Kyiv-declared ceasefire begins
item.description: Ukraine said Russia had continued to launch drone and missile attacks into the country on Wednesday morning, hours after a unilateral ceasefire declared by Kyiv had come into effect. Both Kyiv and Moscow announced competing ceasefires on different dates this week as Russia prepares to celebrate the anniversary of the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
